correct.label: Correct strategy
correct.description: Measure both fractions in a shared unit before combining.

incorrect.label: Incorrect strategy
incorrect.description: Add the visible numerator and denominator components as if they were independent counts.

frames.caption: 1/3 + 1/4. The parts are not the same size.
Add numerator to numerator and denominator to denominator: 2/7. This ignores the shared unit; co-measurement gives 7/12.

frame_sequence.caption: 1/3 + 1/4. The parts are not the same size.
Add numerator to numerator and denominator to denominator: 2/7. This ignores the shared unit; co-measurement gives 7/12.

visuals.expression: circle partition transplanted onto a rectangle

correct.label: Correct strategy
correct.description: Partition the rectangular area model by its own part-of-part vocabulary.

frames.caption: Split the unit square into 2 columns; select 1 (1/2 one way).
Split the other way into 3 rows; select 1 (1/3 the other way).
The overlap is 1 of 6 small rectangles: 1/2 x 1/3 = fraction(1,6).

grounding.metaphor_gloss: Object Construction — numbers as wholes built from parts

breaks_at.reason: Zero requires the ad-hoc Zero Object Metaphor; lack-of-object is not itself a constructible object.
No source-domain referent for 'an object that is the absence of an object'; requires the ad-hoc Zero Object Metaphor.
Irrationals are not constructible by finite assembly of unit parts.

teacher.embodied: Fit equal parts together into a whole object.
teacher.standard: fraction strand: area model part of part

incorrect.label: Incorrect strategy
incorrect.description: Move the circle's radial partition rule onto the rectangle host.

frames.caption: Choose a rectangle as the area-model host.
The radial partition is licensed inside the circle-region vocabulary.
Transplant the circle partition rule onto the rectangle host.
The result is a hybridized model: a foreign primitive on an illicit host.

frame_sequence.caption: Split the unit square into 2 columns; select 1 (1/2 one way).
Split the other way into 3 rows; select 1 (1/3 the other way).
The overlap is 1 of 6 small rectangles: 1/2 x 1/3 = fraction(1,6).
Choose a rectangle as the area-model host.
The radial partition is licensed inside the circle-region vocabulary.
Transplant the circle partition rule onto the rectangle host.
The result is a hybridized model: a foreign primitive on an illicit host.

correct.label: Correct strategy
correct.description: Preserve equality by making the same move on both pans.

frames.caption: 2x + 3 = 11. The two sides name the same weight, so the beam is level.
Take 3 units off BOTH pans. Left 2x, right 8; still level.
Split each pan into 2 equal groups: one x-box balances 8 / 2 = 4 units.
x = 4. One x-box balances 4 units; the beam stays level.

incorrect.label: Incorrect strategy
incorrect.description: Treat the equals sign as a command and change only one side.

frames.caption: 2x + 3 = 11. The two sides name the same weight, so the beam is level.
Taking 3 off the LEFT pan only leaves 2x against 11: the beam tips. The same move was not done to both sides.

doc.note: Solving keeps the beam level by doing the same move to both pans; computing one side tips it.

frame_sequence.caption: 2x + 3 = 11. The two sides name the same weight, so the beam is level.
Take 3 units off BOTH pans. Left 2x, right 8; still level.
Split each pan into 2 equal groups: one x-box balances 8 / 2 = 4 units.
x = 4. One x-box balances 4 units; the beam stays level.
